problem: I have 6 shirts, 6 pairs of pants, and 6 hats.  Each item comes in the same 6 colors (so that I have one of each item of each color).  I refuse to wear an outfit in which all 3 items are the same color.  How many choices for outfits do I have?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the total number of outfit combinations possible, considering that you have 6 shirts, 6 pairs of pants, and 6 hats, each in 6 different colors?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many outfits would there be if you didn't have the restriction of not wearing an outfit in which all three items are the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the number of outfits where all three items are the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Using the information given, can you determine the number of outfits where not all three items are the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the relationship between the total number of outfit combinations and the number of outfits where not all three items are the same color?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other scenarios or problems where a similar thought process or steps would be used to find the solution?
classified_questions.label: e
